source.name: Open Scriptures Hebrew Bible / Westminster Leningrad Codex line

JSON (to be confirmed from official source documentation)
Plain text (to be confirmed from official source documentation)

licensing.textLicense: Public domain (current understanding; to be confirmed from official source documentation)
licensing.annotationLicense: CC BY 4.0 for lemma/morphology layers (current understanding; to be confirmed from official source documentation)
notes: Do not treat licensing assumptions as final until upstream license text is recorded.
Annotation layers must remain separable until license compatibility is confirmed for downstream bundling.

technical.verseNumbering: Preserve source chapter/verse numbering exactly as provided by the selected upstream dataset.
normalizationNotes: Normalization policy for niqqud and cantillation is not yet fixed.
Keep raw files immutable and record any transformation profile applied to processed outputs.
parserNotes: Importer should maintain stable verse identifiers tied to source numbering.
If alternate numbering systems are needed, create explicit mapping metadata rather than mutating source references.
Do not merge external annotation layers by default; keep attribution and provenance attached per layer.

intendedUse: Canonical source record for Hebrew Bible imports
Input for importer configuration and validation
Reference metadata for reader and study tooling

openQuestions: Which exact upstream repository or distribution URL will Sacred pin as canonical?
What exact release identifier (tag/commit/date) will be used for the first import?
Which file format should be selected as Sacred's canonical raw format?
What explicit attribution wording is required for annotation layers, if used?
How should Hebrew/Jewish to common English Christian verse mapping be represented and versioned?

notes: This manifest is intentionally conservative and avoids unverified claims.
Update this file before any production import or redistribution of Hebrew Bible source material.
